seo.title: Chumphon Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Chumphon travel guide for 2026. Koh Tao ferry schedule, 3-day itinerary, diving, local food guide, budget breakdown, and practical transport tips for Thailand's Gulf gateway.

intro: Chumphon is where southern Thailand properly begins — where the peninsula narrows and the Gulf coast reveals its first hint of the tropical island scenery that waits further south. Most travelers know it only as the overnight train stop before catching a ferry to Koh Tao. But if you slow down, Chumphon has genuine rewards: empty beaches, one of Thailand's great dive sites, a working fishing port where you can eat fresh blue crab at quayside restaurants, and a pace of life that disappeared from the famous islands decades ago. This guide covers the practical details for doing Chumphon properly — getting there, getting around, what to eat, and how to reach Koh Tao on your own schedule.

itinerary.title: 3 Days in Chumphon: Beach, Diving & Local Life

days.title: Arrive, Orient & Hat Thung Wua Laen Beach
activities: Morning: Arrive by overnight train from Bangkok (departs Hua Lamphong ~19:30, arrives Chumphon ~03:30-05:00) or by daytime train/bus
Rent a motorbike from a shop near the train station (200-250 THB/day) — you'll need it for the next three days
Breakfast: Local morning market on Sala Daeng Road — try kanom jeen (fermented rice noodles with southern curry sauce) for 30-40 THB
Late morning: Ride 20 km north to Hat Thung Wua Laen Beach — check in to your beachfront guesthouse
Afternoon: Swim, snorkel just off the beach, and walk the full 3-km stretch of sand to understand why this is Chumphon's finest beach
Evening: Sunset from the beach, then dinner at one of the beachfront seafood restaurants — try grilled whole fish (pla pao) and grilled squid (pla muek yang) for 150-300 THB per dish
If you're a diver: Book a Chumphon Pinnacle day trip at the resort dive desk for tomorrow morning

days.title: Dive Chumphon Pinnacle or Island Snorkeling
activities: Option A — Diving: Depart Hat Thung Wua Laen at 8:00 AM by dive boat to Chumphon Pinnacle (23 km offshore). Two dives (2,500-3,500 THB with gear) — an underwater seamount with massive schools of pelagic fish, possible whale shark sightings March-May. Return by 1:00-2:00 PM

Option B — Snorkeling: Day trip from Pak Nam Chumphon pier to Ko Ngam Noi and Ko Talu (800-1,500 THB + 200 THB park fee). White-sand beaches, healthy coral, and crystal-clear water in the national park islands with almost no other tourists
Afternoon: Return to Hat Thung Wua Laen for recovery time and a beach nap
Late afternoon: Ride your motorbike south to Khao Matsi Viewpoint (5 km southeast of Chumphon town) for a panoramic sunset view over the estuary and Hat Pharadon Phap beach
Evening: Head to Chumphon Night Market on Kromluang Chumphon Road for dinner — 150-250 THB for a full meal at local prices

days.title: Pak Nam Seafood & Onward to Koh Tao
activities: Early morning (7-9 AM): Ride to Pak Nam Chumphon (11 km southeast) for the morning fish market — boats returning with the night's catch, the most atmospheric time at the port
Breakfast: Fresh seafood breakfast at a quayside restaurant — steamed blue crab, grilled fish, and jasmine rice for 150-300 THB per person
Mid-morning: Walk the Pak Nam waterfront, visit the Prince Chumphon memorial shrine on the seafront
Late morning: If heading to Koh Tao on the 1:30 PM ferry — ride back to Chumphon town, return the motorbike, collect your luggage
Afternoon: Lomprayah shuttle bus from Tha Tapao Road office to the pier at 12:00 PM (50 THB) — 1:30 PM departure to Koh Tao, arriving 3:15 PM
Alternative: Stay another night, rise at 5:30 AM, and catch the 7:00 AM Lomprayah ferry to Koh Tao for a full day arrival

transport.fromBangkok: By train (recommended): Overnight sleeper from Hua Lamphong station (train 167/169) departs around 19:00-19:30, arrives Chumphon 03:30-05:00 — second-class sleeper costs 700-1,200 THB. Daytime express takes 7-8 hours from 300 THB (third class) to 600 THB (second class AC). By bus: Southern Bus Terminal (Sai Tai Mai) to Chumphon, 7-8 hours, from 350 THB (ordinary) to 600 THB (VIP). By car: 480 km south on Highway 4 (Phetkasem Road), approximately 5.5-6 hours. Flights: Bangkok Airways occasionally operates via Surat Thani; no direct budget airline service to Chumphon's small airport.
localTransport: Motorbike rental: 200-250 THB/day from shops near the train station on Sala Daeng Road — essential for reaching Hat Thung Wua Laen (20 km), Pak Nam (11 km), and viewpoints. International driving permit technically required but rarely checked
Lomprayah shuttle bus: 50 THB from the Tha Tapao Road office to Lomprayah Pier (7 km), departing 90 minutes before each sailing. Take this rather than negotiating with tuk-tuk drivers
Songthaew (shared pickup trucks): Infrequent routes within Chumphon town for 15-30 THB. Occasional service to Hat Thung Wua Laen for 60-80 THB — ask at your guesthouse for schedules
Motorbike taxis: Available near the train station and market area for short hops within town — 30-60 THB for most town destinations. Negotiate before boarding
Tuk-tuks: Less common than in larger cities but available near the train station — 80-150 THB for town routes
Taxi (private): For Hat Thung Wua Laen and Pak Nam trips, guesthouses can arrange a private taxi or pickup for 400-600 THB one-way
transport.ferryToKohTao: Lomprayah High Speed Catamaran: Departs Lomprayah Pier (7 km south of town) at 7:00 AM and 1:30 PM daily. Journey: 1 hour 45 minutes to Mae Haad Pier, Koh Tao. Price: 750 THB one-way. Book at lomprayah.com or any travel agency in town. Combo tickets to Koh Phangan and Koh Samui available. Songserm also operates a slower overnight ferry from Chumphon to Koh Tao (8 hours, 400 THB) — less comfortable but useful if daytime ferries are fully booked.

mustTry: Pu nue (blue crab) — Chumphon is renowned throughout Thailand for the quality of its Gulf blue crab. Order steamed with nam jim talay (seafood dipping sauce) at Pak Nam quayside restaurants. A medium whole crab costs 200-400 THB
Kanom jeen nam ya — fermented rice noodles served with southern Thai fish curry sauce. A staple breakfast at Chumphon's morning market for 30-40 THB per bowl

Pla pao (grilled whole fish) — fresh-caught fish packed with lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves, wrapped in banana leaf and grilled over charcoal. Order at Hat Thung Wua Laen beachfront restaurants for 200-350 THB
Pla muek yang (grilled squid) — Chumphon's squid is some of the freshest on the Gulf coast, grilled on sticks at the night market and beach restaurants for 80-150 THB
Khao man gai (Hainanese chicken rice) — a Thai staple done well at Chumphon's market stalls and night market vendors for 50-70 THB
Ma phrao (fresh coconut) — cold young coconut from street vendors at the market and beach for 20-30 THB — essential refreshment in the heat
foodAreas: Pak Nam Chumphon quayside (6-10 AM and 4-7 PM) — the freshest and cheapest seafood in the province. Blue crab, grilled fish, steamed squid at local fishing community prices
Chumphon Night Market, Kromluang Chumphon Road (daily 5-9 PM) — the widest variety of food in the province at the lowest prices. Start here on your first evening
Sala Daeng Road morning market (6-10 AM) — local Thai breakfast. Kanom jeen, khao tom (rice porridge), fried dough (patongo), and fresh fruit
Hat Thung Wua Laen beachfront restaurants (11 AM-10 PM) — tourist-priced by Chumphon standards but still affordable. Fresh seafood in a Gulf sunrise or sunset setting
tips: Southern Thai food is significantly spicier than Bangkok Thai — tell vendors 'mai pet' (not spicy) if you can't handle heat, or 'pet nit noi' (a little spicy)
Chumphon's seafood quality is the province's greatest asset — eat as much crab, squid, and fresh fish as possible. It's far fresher and cheaper here than in Bangkok or on Koh Tao
The night market is open every day, not just weekends — reliable cheap eating for all three meals of your stay
Don't eat at the train station — walk 10 minutes to the morning market for the same quality at half the price

etiquette: Cover shoulders and knees at Wat Phra Borommathat and any Buddhist temple — sarongs are sometimes available but bring your own to be safe
Remove shoes before entering temple buildings and any Thai home you're invited into
At the Pak Nam fishing pier, ask permission before photographing fishing families and their boats — most will agree, but asking is respectful
Chumphon province has Muslim communities particularly in the south — dress modestly away from the beach areas
Tipping is not expected but appreciated — 20-50 THB for restaurant meals, 50-100 THB for boat captains and guides
Thai national anthem plays publicly at 8 AM and 6 PM — stand still as a sign of respect if you're in a public space

budget.budget: 600-1,200 THB/day (~$17-34 USD): Town guesthouse 500-900 THB, market meals 150-300 THB, motorbike rental 200-250 THB/day, free beaches and viewpoints. Chumphon is extremely budget-friendly by Thai beach standards — you can eat excellent fresh seafood and stay on the beach for less than most Bangkok hotels.
budget.midRange: 1,500-3,500 THB/day (~$42-98 USD): Hat Thung Wua Laen resort 1,500-3,000 THB, mix of beachfront restaurants and night market 400-700 THB, one activity (island snorkeling 800-1,500 THB or diving 2,500-3,500 THB), motorbike rental 200-250 THB. Good value compared to equivalent quality on Koh Tao or Koh Samui.
budget.luxury: 4,000-8,000+ THB/day (~$112-224 USD): Best mid-range beachfront resort 2,500-3,500 THB, seafood restaurant dining 500-1,000 THB, private boat charter for island day trip 4,000-6,000 THB, dive trips. Note: No international luxury chain hotels exist in Chumphon — the highest-end option is Chumphon Cabana which is comfortable mid-range rather than true luxury.
